real_meaning: Contains artificial sweeteners or hidden Maltodextrin that can spike blood sugar worse than real sugar.
biological_impact: Maltodextrin is a highly processed powder that breaks down into sugar instantly. It hits your bloodstream like a freight train, causing a massive insulin spike that is actually higher than if you had just eaten a spoon of regular table sugar.
how_to_spot: Look at the ingredient list on the back. If you see 'Maltodextrin', 'Sucralose', 'Aspartame', or 'INS 955 / INS 951', it is chemically sweetened.
evidenceStatement: ✅ EVIDENCE: Clinical glycemic index (GI) tables confirm Maltodextrin has a GI of 105-136, significantly higher than sucrose (GI 65).
clinicalDisclaimer: 🚨 DANGER: Diabetic patients should be extremely cautious of 'Sugar-Free' products containing Maltodextrin as they can cause unexpected, dangerous blood sugar spikes.

real_meaning: Usually 90% refined white flour (Maida) with a tiny sprinkling of cheap seeds on top to look healthy.
biological_impact: Refined flour has all the healthy fiber and vitamins completely stripped away. Inside your body, it acts exactly like eating pure sugar, leading to rapid fat storage around your belly and a crash in your energy levels two hours later.
how_to_spot: Check the very first ingredient on the back. If it says 'Refined Wheat Flour' or 'Maida', it is junk food. A true healthy bread will list '100% Whole Wheat Flour' as the FIRST and ONLY flour.
evidenceStatement: ✅ EVIDENCE: Nutritional guidelines define refined carbohydrates as simple sugars that rapidly convert to glucose, driving hyperinsulinemia.
clinicalDisclaimer: 📍 IMPORTANT: The color brown in bread is often just caramel coloring (INS 150), not whole grain fiber.

real_meaning: A sugar-filled junk drink with a microscopic, artificial spray of synthetic Vitamin C.
biological_impact: Massive amounts of sugar actually paralyze your immune system. Sugar stops your white blood cells from eating bacteria for up to 5 hours after you drink it. This completely cancels out any tiny benefit the artificial vitamin might have given you.
how_to_spot: Check the 'Total Sugars' per 100g on the nutrition panel. If it is over 10g, the product will harm your immunity, no matter what cartoon shield is on the front box.
evidenceStatement: ✅ EVIDENCE: Studies show simple sugars reduce the phagocytic index (bacteria-eating ability) of neutrophils.
clinicalDisclaimer: 📍 IMPORTANT: You cannot 'boost' immunity with sugar-laden products; you can only suppress it.

real_meaning: Contains less than 5% fruit puree or concentrate, functioning mainly as a flavor vehicle for sugar water.
biological_impact: Fruit concentrate is essentially just fruit sugar (fructose) with all the healthy fiber boiled away. Without fiber, fructose goes straight to your liver, where it is instantly converted into visceral fat (belly fat) and can cause fatty liver disease.
how_to_spot: Read the ingredient percentages. If 'Fruit Pulp/Concentrate' is listed at 2% or 5%, you are paying a premium price for flavored sugar water.
evidenceStatement: ✅ EVIDENCE: The WHO strongly recommends limiting free sugars, specifically grouping fruit juice concentrates with added sugars due to the lack of intact cellular fiber.
clinicalDisclaimer: 📍 IMPORTANT: Eating a whole piece of fruit is completely different and much healthier than drinking its concentrated juice.

real_meaning: Still highly processed junk, often sprayed with cheap, low-quality inflammatory seed oils after baking.
biological_impact: Baked snacks are often made of puffed corn or rice that melts in your mouth—meaning it hits your bloodstream instantly as sugar. Even worse, they are often heavily coated in cheap Palmolein or Refined Sunflower oil, which causes silent inflammation throughout your entire body.
how_to_spot: Look for 'Edible Vegetable Oil (Palmolein)' or 'Refined Oil' in the ingredients. Check the calorie count; a bag of 'baked' diet chips often has the exact same calories as regular fried chips.
evidenceStatement: ✅ EVIDENCE: Extruded (puffed/baked) grain snacks have extremely high glycemic indices, and high omega-6 seed oils drive systemic inflammatory markers like CRP.
clinicalDisclaimer: 📍 IMPORTANT: 'Baked' simply refers to the cooking method. It does not mean low-calorie or healthy.

real_meaning: Uses a legal loophole allowing up to 0.5g of trans fat per serving by shrinking the serving size to a ridiculously small amount.
biological_impact: Trans fats are artificial, plastic-like fats that your body literally cannot digest. Instead of burning for energy, they permanently stick to the inside walls of your blood vessels, clogging your arteries like old cooking grease and causing heart attacks.
how_to_spot: Ignore the 'Zero' claim on the front. If the ingredients list 'Partially Hydrogenated Vegetable Oil', 'Hydrogenated Fat', or 'Vanaspati', it contains dangerous trans fats.
evidenceStatement: ✅ EVIDENCE: The American Heart Association states there is no safe level of artificial trans fat consumption, as it uniquely raises LDL (bad) and lowers HDL (good) cholesterol.
clinicalDisclaimer: 🚨 DANGER: Even small amounts of trans fats consumed daily accumulate to cause severe cardiovascular disease.

real_meaning: Uses the cheapest, lowest-quality synthetic vitamins that your body cannot even absorb, just so they can print it on the label.
biological_impact: Companies use cheap chemicals like Calcium Carbonate (which is basically chalk) instead of forms your body can actually use. Most of these 'added vitamins' pass straight through your digestive system into the toilet, while the product delivers a massive dose of sugar.
how_to_spot: Look at the specific chemical names in brackets next to the vitamins on the back. If it says 'Ferrous Fumarate' (cheap iron) or 'Calcium Carbonate' (cheap calcium), the company is cutting corners.
evidenceStatement: ✅ EVIDENCE: Bioavailability studies show significant differences in absorption rates between different salt forms of minerals (e.g., citrate vs. carbonate).
clinicalDisclaimer: 📍 IMPORTANT: Do not rely on fortified junk food for your daily vitamins. Real, whole foods are always superior.

real_meaning: Derived from a natural source originally, but heavily processed in a chemical lab using strong solvents.
biological_impact: While legally allowed to be called 'natural', these extracts are heavily manipulated. Some 'natural' colors like Carmine (INS 120, made from crushed bugs) or Annatto (INS 160b) can still trigger severe allergic reactions or stomach cramps in sensitive people.
how_to_spot: Look for generic terms like 'Added Flavors (Natural & Nature Identical)' or INS numbers. 'Nature Identical' simply means it was chemically built in a lab to mimic a natural molecule.
evidenceStatement: ✅ EVIDENCE: The FDA defines 'natural flavors' broadly, allowing for extensive chemical extraction and the inclusion of up to 80% synthetic solvents/carriers in the final flavoring mixture.
clinicalDisclaimer: 📍 IMPORTANT: 'Natural' on a food label is a marketing term with very little strict regulatory definition regarding health.

real_meaning: Contains mostly cheap liquid glucose or invert sugar, with just a tiny drop of honey added so they can put a picture of a bee on the box.
biological_impact: Even if the product used 100% real jaggery, your liver cannot tell the difference between jaggery and refined white sugar. They both break down into the exact same glucose and fructose molecules, causing the exact same fat storage and insulin spikes.
how_to_spot: Check the ingredient list order. Ingredients are listed by weight. If 'Invert Sugar', 'Liquid Glucose', or 'Sugar' comes BEFORE 'Honey' or 'Jaggery', you're being scammed.
evidenceStatement: ✅ EVIDENCE: Endocrinologists confirm that all caloric sweeteners (honey, jaggery, maple syrup, cane sugar) elicit nearly identical insulin responses and caloric loads per gram.
clinicalDisclaimer: 📍 IMPORTANT: Replacing white sugar with jaggery or honey does NOT make a dessert healthy or diabetic-friendly.

real_meaning: Uses cheap, low-quality plant protein like soy isolate, or requires you to drink two huge glasses of milk just to reach the advertised protein number.
biological_impact: Cheap plant protein isolates often lack the complete set of amino acids your body needs to actually build muscle. Furthermore, these products are often so packed with sugar that the sugar spike completely ruins any metabolic benefit the protein might have provided.
how_to_spot: Look at the 'Protein per 100g' row. Anything less than 15g-20g per 100g is NOT a high protein food. Also check if the box says 'Protein *when consumed with 200ml milk'.
evidenceStatement: ✅ EVIDENCE: Muscle protein synthesis (MPS) requires a complete essential amino acid profile (specifically Leucine), which is often deficient in cheap plant isolates.
clinicalDisclaimer: 📍 IMPORTANT: Read the fine print. Many products claim 'High Protein' based on the milk you add to them, not the powder itself.
